# DrifterApps.Seeds.FluentScenario

A C# library for writing expressive BDD (Behavior-Driven Development) test scenarios using a fluent Given-When-Then API.

## NuGet Packages

```
DrifterApps.Seeds.FluentScenario                  # Core library
DrifterApps.Seeds.FluentScenario.FluentAssertions # Optional: FluentAssertions integration
```

Targets: net10.0

## Core Concepts

### ScenarioRunner
The entry point. Create one per test, chain steps, then call `PlayAsync()` to execute them sequentially.

### Ensure<T>
A readonly struct that wraps values passed between steps. Carries a validity flag so steps can safely check whether input arrived. Use `.IsValid` to check, `.Value` to unwrap (throws if invalid), or implicit conversion to `T`.

### IStepRunner
A sub-runner passed into `Given(Action<IStepRunner>)` overloads. Lets you group related sub-steps inside a single BDD keyword and share context between them.

### Context
`SetContextData(key, value)` / `GetContextData<T>(key)` — both `ScenarioRunner` and `IStepRunner` implement `IRunnerContext`. Use this to share state when not using return-value chaining.

### IScenarioOutput
The output sink for step results. xUnit: wrap `ITestOutputHelper`. Implement the interface for other frameworks.

## Choosing a Pattern

| Situation | Pattern to use |
|---|---|
| Quick inline scenario | Inline lambdas with explicit descriptions |
| Reusable steps across tests | `IStepRunner` methods |
| Self-documenting method names as descriptions | `CallerMemberName` overloads |
| Passing values between steps type-safely | `Ensure<T>` return-value chaining |
| Sharing data without return values | `SetContextData` / `GetContextData<T>` |

## Pattern 1 — Inline lambdas

Steps defined directly in the chain. Pass a string description.

```csharp
[Fact]
public async Task WeatherScenario()
{
    var temperatureInside = 0;
    var temperatureOutside = 0;

    await ScenarioRunner.Create("when the weather is too cold", _output)
        .Given("I want to go play outside", () => temperatureInside = 19)
        .When("the temperature is below 0C", () => temperatureOutside = -5)
        .Then("I stay inside if the difference is greater than 20C",
            () => (temperatureInside - temperatureOutside).Should().BeGreaterThan(20))
        .PlayAsync();
}
```

## Pattern 2 — IStepRunner methods (reusable steps)

Pass a method that accepts `IStepRunner`. Call `runner.Execute(description, action)` inside. Steps can share state via context.

```csharp
[Fact]
public async Task WeatherScenario()
{
    await ScenarioRunner.Create("when the weather is too cold", _output)
        .Given(IWantToGoPlayOutside)
        .When(TheTemperatureIsBelow0C)
        .Then(IStayInsideIfDifferenceIsGreaterThan20C)
        .PlayAsync();
}

private static void IWantToGoPlayOutside(IStepRunner runner) =>
    runner.Execute("I want to go play outside", () =>
        runner.SetContextData("temperatureInside", 19));

private static void TheTemperatureIsBelow0C(IStepRunner runner) =>
    runner.Execute("the temperature is below 0C", () =>
        runner.SetContextData("temperatureOutside", -5));

private static void IStayInsideIfDifferenceIsGreaterThan20C(IStepRunner runner) =>
    runner.Execute("I stay inside if the difference is greater than 20C", () =>
    {
        var inside = runner.GetContextData<int>("temperatureInside");
        var outside = runner.GetContextData<int>("temperatureOutside");
        (inside - outside).Should().BeGreaterThan(20);
    });
```

## Pattern 3 — CallerMemberName (method name = description)

Omit the description string and use the method name overload. The method name is converted from camelCase to a sentence automatically (`TheTemperatureIsBelow0C` → "the temperature is below 0 c").

```csharp
[Fact]
public async Task WeatherScenario()
{
    await ScenarioRunner.Create(_output)  // no description string → method name used
        .Given(IWantToGoPlayOutside)
        .When(TheTemperatureIsBelow0C)
        .Then(IStayInsideIfDifferenceIsGreaterThan20C)
        .PlayAsync();
}

// Same step methods as Pattern 2, but use Execute() without a description string:
private static void IWantToGoPlayOutside(IStepRunner runner) =>
    runner.Execute(() => runner.SetContextData("temperatureInside", 19));
```

## Pattern 4 — Ensure<T> value chaining

Pass a typed input to `ScenarioRunner.Create<T>()`. Each step can return a value that the next step receives as `Ensure<T>`. Use the FluentAssertions extension `.Should().BeValid()` to assert validity.

```csharp
[Theory]
[InlineData(19)]
public async Task WeatherScenario(int inside)
{
    await ScenarioRunner.Create("when the weather is too cold", inside, _output)
        .Given("I want to go play outside", (Ensure<int> temperatureInside) =>
        {
            temperatureInside.Should().BeValid();
            return (temperatureInside.Value, Outside: -5);
        })
        .When("the temperature difference is great",
            (Ensure<(int Inside, int Outside)> temperatures) =>
            {
                temperatures.Should().BeValid();
                return temperatures.Value.Inside - temperatures.Value.Outside;
            })
        .Then("I stay inside if the difference is greater than 20C",
            (Ensure<int> difference) =>
                difference.Should().BeValid()
                    .And.Subject.Value.Should().BeGreaterThan(20))
        .PlayAsync();
}
```

## IScenarioOutput — Framework Adapters

### xUnit
```csharp
public sealed class XUnitScenarioOutput(ITestOutputHelper output) : IScenarioOutput
{
    public void WriteLine(string message) => output.WriteLine(message);
    public void WriteLine(string format, params object[] args) => output.WriteLine(format, args);
}
```

### NUnit / MSTest / other
Implement the two `WriteLine` methods pointing at your framework's logger.

## API Quick Reference

### ScenarioRunner.Create — factory overloads
```csharp
ScenarioRunner.Create(string description, IScenarioOutput output)
ScenarioRunner.Create(IScenarioOutput output, [CallerMemberName] string description = "")
ScenarioRunner.Create<T>(string description, T input, IScenarioOutput output)
ScenarioRunner.Create<T>(T input, IScenarioOutput output, [CallerMemberName] string description = "")
```

### Step methods — Given / When / Then / And
All four keywords share identical overload sets. Replace `Given` with `When`, `Then`, or `And` as needed.

```csharp
// No input, no output
.Given("description", () => { })
.Given("description", async () => { })
.Given(() => { })                          // CallerMemberName
.Given(async () => { })                   // CallerMemberName

// No input, with output
.Given<T>("description", () => value)
.Given<T>("description", async () => value)
.Given<T>(() => value)                    // CallerMemberName

// With Ensure<T> input, no output
.Given<T>("description", (Ensure<T> input) => { })
.Given<T>("description", async (Ensure<T> input) => { })
.Given<T>((Ensure<T> input) => { })      // CallerMemberName

// With Ensure<T> input, with output
.Given<T, T2>("description", (Ensure<T> input) => value)
.Given<T, T2>("description", async (Ensure<T> input) => value)
.Given<T, T2>((Ensure<T> input) => value) // CallerMemberName

// IStepRunner delegation
.Given(runner => { runner.Execute(...); })
```

### IStepRunner.Execute — same overload set
```csharp
runner.Execute("description", () => { })
runner.Execute("description", async () => { })
runner.Execute<T>("description", (Ensure<T> input) => { })
runner.Execute<T>("description", () => value)
runner.Execute<T, T2>("description", (Ensure<T> input) => value)
// ... and CallerMemberName variants of each
```

### Execution
```csharp
await runner.PlayAsync();          // Execute all steps; throws on first failure
await runner.PlayAsync<T>();       // Execute and return final step result as Ensure<T>
```

### Ensure<T>
```csharp
bool isValid = ensure.IsValid;
T value = ensure.Value;            // throws InvalidOperationException if !IsValid
T implicit = ensure;               // implicit operator
Ensure<T> wrapped = someValue;     // implicit operator

// FluentAssertions (requires FluentScenario.FluentAssertions package)
ensure.Should().BeValid();
ensure.Should().BeInvalid();
ensure.Should().HaveValue(expected);
ensure.Should().BeNull();
ensure.Should().NotBeNull();
```

### Context
```csharp
runner.SetContextData("key", value);
T value = runner.GetContextData<T>("key");
```

## Common Mistakes

- **Missing `await PlayAsync()`** — steps are registered lazily; nothing runs until `PlayAsync()` is called.
- **Accessing `Ensure<T>.Value` without checking `IsValid`** — throws `InvalidOperationException` when the input did not arrive (e.g., first step with no `Create<T>` input). Always assert or check first.
- **Using `GetContextData<T>` with wrong type** — will throw at runtime. Keep keys and types consistent.
- **Mixing return-value chaining and context storage in the same scenario** — pick one approach per scenario for clarity.
